channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: A Nuclear Debut in Bangladesh Tests Developing World’s Atomic Shift
item.description: A new, $13 billion Russian-built plant could generate 15% of the country's electricity.
item.dc:creator: Rajesh Kumar Singh, Arun Devnath

item.title: Another Brutal Summer Tests Arizona’s Decline in Heat-Linked Deaths
item.description: Maricopa County has made progress in preventing heat-related deaths. But another scorching summer and dwindling funding threaten that trend.
item.dc:creator: Zahra Hirji

item.title: Lush Grass Playing Fields for Tennis, Cricket Struggle in UK Heat
item.description: Heat waves are baking famous sports grounds.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Rudgard, Eric Ombok, Adelaide Changole

item.title: Relentless Heat Waves Hit England’s Fabled Grass Playing Fields
item.description: When the England cricket team walks onto the field at Lord’s for a home Test, it’s to the strains of “Jerusalem,” an anthem based on a William Blake poem that extols “England’s green and pleasant land.”
item.dc:creator: Olivia Rudgard

item.title: China Power Load Hits Early Record as Data, EV Demand Rises
item.description: China’s nationwide electricity load reached a record high for the first time this year on July 10, hitting 1.518 billion kilowatts, the National Energy Administration said in a statement posted on its official WeChat account Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Zhu, Ying Tian, Dan Murtaugh

item.title: Typhoon Bavi Tracks Toward China With Heavy, Widespread Rains
item.description: A large and powerful typhoon is moving toward China, bringing heavy rain and strong winds ahead of its expected landfall later Saturday and triggering an emergency response in the Zhejiang province.
item.dc:creator: Mary Hui

item.title: US Settles With Keystone Pipeline Owner Over 2022 Oil Spill
item.description: The Trump administration and the state of Kansas have settled with the owner and operator of the Keystone Pipeline over alleged violations of the Clean Water Act stemming from a 2022 oil spill.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Kolson Hurley, Zahra Hirji

item.title: Families in Europe’s Scorched Cities Are Being Tested By Heat
item.description: Parents face difficult choices to protect children amid punishing extreme temperatures
item.dc:creator: Laura Millan, Akshat Rathi, Summer Maxwell

item.title: Western US Heat Builds as Storms Threaten Plains to Mid-Atlantic
item.description: Heat is spreading across the western US, with the most intense conditions concentrated in the central and northern Rocky Mountains and the northern Great Plains. Heat advisories, warnings and watches stretch from Nevada to northeastern Minnesota, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Brian K Sullivan

item.title: German Lawmakers Pass Rollback of Controversial Heating Law
item.description: German lawmakers approved a rollback of a controversial law that encouraged a shift away from fossil-fuel heating systems, likely extending the lifetime of more polluting boilers.
item.dc:creator: Petra Sorge
